title: Lemon balm tincture
subtitle: A nerve-calming herb herbal tincture for tension and digestive nerves
excerpt: Dried lemon balm leaf soaked in 40% vodka for 4-6 weeks. A gentle nerve-calming herb herbal tincture for mild tension, nervous stomach, and restlessness. Suitable for daytime use at low dose without drowsiness.
sourceNotes: Maud Grieve (the early 20th-century botanical writer), A Modern Herbal, lemon balm entry: the nerve-calming herb and wind-easing herb tradition, the traditional use for nervous digestion and restlessness. the herbal reference on Melissae folium, the well-established use for mild anxiety and sleep, and the traditional use for digestive nervous conditions.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Macerate 4-6 weeks in a cool dark cupboard. Strain and bottle. Keeps 2 years in amber glass.

projectSchedule.title: Pack the jar and cover with vodka
projectSchedule.body: Pack dried lemon balm into the soaking jar. Pour over vodka to cover the herb by 2 cm. Seal, label, and shake. Place in a cool dark cupboard.

projectSchedule.title: Strain and bottle this spirit extraction
projectSchedule.body: Strain through muslin, pressing the herb firmly. Bottle in amber glass. The herbal tincture should be pale green-gold and smell distinctly lemony and herbal.

glossaryTerms.definition: A polyphenol found in lemon balm (and rosemary, sage, and other Lamiaceae). Associated with the nervine and antiviral actions in lemon balm; it inhibits certain enzymes involved in the breakdown of calming neurotransmitters.

content.text: Lemon balm (Melissa officinalis) is a gentle calming herb. It is safe to take during the day. Its

and aromatic oil (citral, citronellal) combine to give it both the calming and digestive action the western tradition draws on. It has a good evidence base for mild anxiety and sleep disturbance. A spirit extraction preserves the herb year-round and is a convenient daytime form.

attrs.title: Important safety notes
attrs.body:
Not medical advice. Consult a qualified herbalist or doctor for ongoing or serious symptoms.

Thyroid medication. High doses of lemon balm have been studied for symptomatic benefit in hyperthyroidism. Readers on thyroid hormone replacement (levothyroxine) should consult their prescriber before regular therapeutic use, as lemon balm may affect thyroid hormone levels.

Alcohol content. Each dose (2-3 ml) contains approximately 0.8-1.2 ml of alcohol from the vodka. Very small in total, but readers who avoid all alcohol should use an infusion or a glycerite instead.

Lemon balm is one of the safest of the nerve-calming herb herbs and has no significant interactions with most medications at standard doses.

Pregnancy. Culinary use and an occasional cup of lemon balm tea are well within the safe range throughout pregnancy. Daily therapeutic herbal tincture use is outside the culinary context; consult your midwife.

items.prepNote: dried lemon balm leaf; fresh lemon balm can be used at twice the weight
plain vodka, 40% ABV; 40% is the standard for lemon balm

content.text: Pack the dried lemon balm loosely into a 500 ml soaking jar. Pour over the vodka to cover the herb by at least 2 cm. Seal and label with the herb, spirit strength, and date. Shake and place in a cool, dark cupboard. Shake daily for 4-6 weeks. At 4 weeks, the spirit should be pale gold with a distinct lemony, citrus-herbal smell. At 6 weeks, the extraction is complete.

Strain through muslin folded double, pressing the spent herb firmly. Bottle in amber dropper bottles, labelled with the herb, date, and dilution (1:5, 40% spirit). Keeps 2 years from the bottling date.

Adults: 2-3 ml in a small glass of water, up to three times a day. For sleep support, take a 3 ml dose 30-60 minutes before bed. For daytime tension, 2 ml is the lower end of the useful range. Take for up to 4-6 weeks continuously. Paediatric use: not for children under twelve without specialist advice.
Amber glass bottles in a cool, dark cupboard. Keeps 2 years from the bottling date.
When not to use this remedy
On thyroid medicine without prescriber approval. Anxiety that affects your daily life needs medical assessment.
